companies.name: NVIDIA
companies.summary: NVIDIA's 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders is tomorrow, June 24 at 9 a.m. PT — the first stockholder meeting since Vera Rubin entered full production and the Korea AI factory agreements were signed. IREN Limited — in which NVIDIA holds up to a $2.1 billion investment option — will join the Russell 3000 alongside CoreWeave effective June 27. No new product announcements were issued June 23. Vera Rubin NVL72 systems remain in production at Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceXAI.

items.headline: NVIDIA Annual Meeting of Stockholders is tomorrow — June 24 at 9 a.m. PT
items.detail: NVIDIA will hold its 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders online on June 24, 2026 at 9 a.m. PT at the virtual shareholder meeting portal. The meeting comes as Vera Rubin systems are in production at major AI labs, Korea AI factory agreements were signed during Jensen Huang's June 5–8 Seoul visit, and NVIDIA's FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue remains on track for approximately $56 billion. Non-stockholders may attend via guest login.

items.headline: IREN joins Russell 3000 alongside CoreWeave effective June 27 — NVIDIA holds $2.1B investment option in IREN
items.detail: IREN Limited (IREN) will join the Russell 3000 Index effective June 27, alongside CoreWeave (CRWV). NVIDIA signed a data-center partnership with IREN that includes an option to invest up to $2.1 billion, extending its equity-anchored infrastructure strategy. Both IREN and CoreWeave are building Vera Rubin–based AI factory capacity, and Russell 3000 inclusion increases passive-fund visibility for NVIDIA's infrastructure investment network.

items.headline: Jensen Huang's Seoul visit produced gigawatt-scale AI factory agreements with SK Telecom, LG Group, SK hynix, NAVER, and Hyundai
items.detail: NVIDIA deepened South Korea alliances across a four-day visit by CEO Jensen Huang (June 5–8), securing agreements spanning gigawatt-scale AI cloud (SK Telecom using NVIDIA DSX, first factory 2027), AI factory for robotics and autonomous driving (LG Group), next-generation HBM supply (SK hynix), sovereign AI infrastructure scaled toward 1 GW (NAVER), and autonomous driving (Hyundai Motor Group). Jensen Huang told reporters a one-gigawatt AI factory costs roughly $60 billion.

companies.name: Alphabet / Google DeepMind
companies.category: Foundation models, search, cloud, and AI research
companies.summary: Gemini 3.5 Flash remains the default model across the consumer app, AI Mode in Search, and Vertex/Gemini API. Gemini 3.5 Pro — committed by CEO Sundar Pichai for a June release at Google I/O — is still in limited Vertex AI enterprise preview with 7 days remaining on the self-imposed June 30 window; prediction markets put on-time odds at 50–55%. Apple's reported $1 billion per year Gemini deal powering a rebuilt Siri AI remains the largest known enterprise distribution contract, with a September 2026 launch on Google Cloud NVIDIA Blackwell infrastructure. Android 17 shipped June 16 with Gemini Omni. John Jumper's departure to Anthropic remains the most notable talent loss.

items.headline: Gemini 3.5 Pro still in limited enterprise preview with 7 days to June 30 self-imposed deadline
items.detail: Gemini 3.5 Pro remains in limited Vertex AI enterprise preview as of June 23 — seven days before CEO Sundar Pichai's self-imposed June release window closes. Prediction markets assign roughly 50–55% probability to an on-time public launch. The model targets a 2-million-token context window, a Deep Think reasoning mode for complex multi-step problems, and frontier multimodal understanding. Expected pricing is approximately $15 per million input tokens and $60 per million output tokens — roughly 10x Gemini 3.5 Flash.

items.headline: Apple's reported $1B/year Gemini deal to power Siri AI launching September 2026 on Google Cloud NVIDIA infrastructure
items.detail: Apple's reported $1 billion per year agreement for Google Gemini to power a rebuilt Siri AI — announced at WWDC 2026 (June 8) — remains Google DeepMind's largest known enterprise model distribution contract. Siri AI is expected to launch September 2026 on Google Cloud infrastructure powered by NVIDIA Blackwell chips, reaching an estimated 2.2 billion active Apple devices. The EU launch is delayed pending unresolved Digital Markets Act regulatory talks.

items.headline: Android 17 launched June 16 with Gemini Omni integration, Lyria 3 music generation, and AudioLM translation
items.detail: Google released Android 17 on June 16 to Pixel 6 through Pixel 10a devices, with Gemini Omni as the core AI upgrade — enabling persistent, context-aware AI assistance across the OS. Android 17 also ships Lyria 3 music generation and AudioLM real-time translation. Third-party AI APIs are now open to developers, allowing ChatGPT, Claude, and other assistants to integrate as alternatives to Gemini on Android devices.

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.category: Cloud AI, copilots, and enterprise software
companies.summary: Microsoft Copilot Cowork usage-based billing has been active since June 19; the July 1 grace period deadline for frontline tenants is 8 days away. Microsoft 365 Business Standard and Business Premium with Copilot become permanent commercial SKUs July 1. Microsoft IQ (Work IQ + Web IQ) is GA across GitHub Copilot, Foundry, and Copilot Studio. Microsoft is exploring hosting a fine-tuned DeepSeek or comparable open-source model as a lower-cost foundation for some Copilot Cowork workloads. No new major announcements were issued June 23.

items.headline: Microsoft Copilot Cowork usage-based billing active; July 1 grace period deadline 8 days away
items.detail: Microsoft Copilot Cowork has transitioned to usage-based pricing with billing active as of June 19; frontline preview tenants have until July 1 — eight days — to complete transition. Microsoft 365 Business Standard with Copilot and Business Premium with Copilot formalize as permanent commercial SKUs on July 1, with a global price update. New Agentic AI certifications AB-100 and AB-620 enter the catalog as legacy Copilot Applied Skills retire June 30.

items.headline: Microsoft IQ GA across GitHub Copilot, Foundry, and Copilot Studio; MAI-Thinking-1 and MAI-Image-2.5 launched at Build
items.detail: Microsoft IQ — combining Work IQ APIs (organizational context inside the M365 trust boundary) and Web IQ (real-time web grounding) — is generally available across GitHub Copilot, Microsoft Foundry, and Copilot Studio as of June 16. MAI-Thinking-1 (35B parameter reasoning model) and MAI-Image-2.5 remain the newest in-house models, launched at Microsoft Build 2026 (June 2–3).

items.headline: Microsoft exploring fine-tuned DeepSeek or open-source model to reduce Copilot Cowork API costs
items.detail: Axios reported Microsoft is evaluating hosting a fine-tuned version of DeepSeek or a comparable open-source model as a lower-cost foundation for some Copilot Cowork workloads — reducing dependency on Anthropic and OpenAI API costs as agentic token consumption scales. The exploration does not affect Claude's position in Microsoft Foundry or GPT-5.5's status as the primary Copilot foundation model.

companies.name: OpenAI
companies.summary: GPT-5.6 prediction markets assign 83–90% probability to a June 22–28 launch window, but no official release materialized on June 23. A release candidate codenamed 'kindle-alpha' was briefly visible on the Design Arena testing platform before being pulled. OpenAI's most recent releases are GPT-5.5-Cyber (85.6% CyberGym benchmark) and the Patch the Planet open-source cybersecurity initiative (with Trail of Bits and HackerOne), both launched June 22. The 42-state AG probe and potential CFO push for a 2027 IPO remain the key pre-IPO headwinds.

items.headline: GPT-5.6 still unreleased on June 23; 'kindle-alpha' briefly visible on Design Arena before being pulled
items.detail: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 did not launch on June 23. A release candidate codenamed 'kindle-alpha' was briefly visible on the Design Arena testing platform before being removed. Prediction markets assign roughly 83–90% probability to a June 22–28 launch. Chief scientist Jakub Pachocki described it as a 'meaningful improvement' with an expected 1.5 million token context window (43% larger than GPT-5.5), a redesigned reward audit pipeline addressing alignment issues, and roughly 10–15% token efficiency gains.

items.headline: OpenAI launched Patch the Planet and GPT-5.5-Cyber for open-source defensive security
items.detail: OpenAI launched 'Patch the Planet' on June 22 — a cybersecurity initiative built with Trail of Bits and HackerOne to find and fix vulnerabilities in widely used open-source software including cURL, Go, Python, and Sigstore. OpenAI also released GPT-5.5-Cyber (85.6% CyberGym benchmark) and a Codex Security plugin for vulnerability scanning inside developer workflows — all under the Daybreak cybersecurity platform.

items.headline: 42-state AG probe filed June 12; CFO reportedly considering 2027 IPO delay; ChatGPT global market share fell below 50%
items.detail: A 42-state attorney general coalition led by NY AG Letitia James opened a formal investigation into OpenAI on June 12, four days after the confidential S-1 filing. CFO Sarah Friar has reportedly expressed concern about operational and compliance readiness, with a potential delay to 2027. Sensor Tower found ChatGPT's global AI assistant market share fell to 46.4% — the first time below 50% — with Gemini at 27.7% and Claude at 10.3%.

companies.name: Anthropic
companies.summary: Day 11 of the Fable 5 and Mythos 5 global suspension. The credit-based pricing model — $10/$50 per million input/output tokens — went live today (June 23) as the free-trial window closed yesterday. Fable 5 remains offline globally; no restoration announcement has been made by Anthropic, Commerce Department, or the White House. Claude experienced a service disruption starting approximately 10:02 a.m. ET on June 23 that resolved later in the day. Anthropic confirmed its science event 'The Briefing: AI for Science' for June 30 at 10 a.m. PST — linked to the John Jumper hire. Revenue run rate surpasses $30 billion; confidential S-1 remains filed.

items.headline: Fable 5 credit billing live today at $10/$50 per million tokens; ban still in effect on day 11
items.detail: Fable 5 and Mythos 5 remain globally suspended as of June 23 (day 11). The credit-based pricing model at $10 per million input tokens and $50 per million output tokens — double Opus 4.8 rates — went live today after the free-trial window closed June 22. Anthropic has not issued updated pricing guidance for suspended-model billing upon restoration. Key remaining milestones: July 8 (biometric ID policy effective — potential US-citizen access path) and August 1 (60-day executive order deadline for NSA/Treasury/CISA AI risk framework).

items.headline: Anthropic confirmed 'The Briefing: AI for Science' virtual event on June 30
items.detail: Anthropic confirmed its science-focused virtual event 'The Briefing: AI for Science' for June 30, 2026 at 10 a.m. PST. The event will feature product and research demonstrations and a pharma/biotech customer showcase. The timing is widely seen as tied to the hire of Nobel laureate John Jumper — co-creator of AlphaFold and 2024 Nobel Prize winner in chemistry — who left Google DeepMind after nine years to join Anthropic (announced June 19–20). Jumper's specific role has not been disclosed.

items.headline: Claude experienced a service disruption June 23; Trump statement has not produced formal ban reversal
items.detail: Claude experienced a service disruption beginning approximately 10:02 a.m. ET on June 23 that resolved later in the day. Separately, Trump's June 20 Axios statement that Anthropic is no longer a national security threat has not yet produced a formal Commerce Department withdrawal of the export control directive. The Globe and Mail reported active deal-making between Anthropic and Trump administration officials toward a joint AI risk framework. Polymarket gives 57% odds of US Fable 5 restoration before July 1.

companies.name: Meta
companies.category: Open models, consumer AI, and infrastructure
companies.summary: Meta launched in-house Meta Glasses today (June 23) — the Fury and Adventurer at $299 and the Starfire (including a Kylie Jenner edition) at $399 — the first AI glasses designed entirely in-house, powered by Meta AI via Muse Spark from day one. Meta controls approximately 80% of the AI glasses market; the new line is immediately available in the US, Canada, UK, and major European markets. Meta Llama 4 continues to draw enterprise evaluation as a self-hostable alternative during the Fable 5 ban. Meta's $115–135 billion 2026 capex guidance is unchanged.

items.headline: Meta launched in-house Meta Glasses today — Fury and Adventurer at $299, Starfire at $399, powered by Muse Spark
items.detail: Meta debuted its in-house Meta Glasses on June 23 — the Fury, Adventurer (both $299), and Starfire/Kylie Jenner edition ($399). Designed entirely in-house rather than through Ray-Ban or Oakley partnerships, the glasses ship with Meta AI powered by Muse Spark from day one and are immediately available in the US, Canada, UK, and major European markets. Meta controls approximately 80% of the AI smart glasses market, with competition incoming from Google/Warby Parker, Samsung/Gentle Monster, and Apple.

items.headline: Meta Llama 4 is the leading enterprise self-hostable alternative during the Fable 5 ban
items.detail: As the Fable 5 and Mythos 5 global suspension enters day 11, Meta Llama 4 has emerged as the primary self-hostable frontier-class model drawing enterprise evaluation. Enterprises seeking to reduce exposure to government model access revocation risk are evaluating Llama 4's open-weights posture as a hedge. Mistral AI's CEO Arthur Mensch has made similar sovereignty arguments; both reflect the industry's awareness of model access risk as a procurement variable following the June 12 suspension.

items.headline: Meta AI Mode on Facebook launched June 15 for 3.2B daily active users; Meta One subscription testing underway
items.detail: Meta AI Mode on Facebook — launched June 15 — lets users ask plain-language questions and receive synthesized answers drawn from public posts, Groups, and Reels for Meta's 3.2 billion daily active users. Meta is also testing a 'Meta One' subscription brand designed to unify Facebook Plus, Instagram Plus, WhatsApp Plus, and forthcoming AI product subscriptions into a single membership tier as part of its push to build subscription revenue alongside advertising while 2026 capex of $115–135 billion scales.

companies.name: Amazon / AWS
companies.summary: AWS Summit New York (June 17–20) concluded with AgentCore GA, AWS Continuum, AWS Context, and EC2 G7 instances as flagship enterprise releases. Amazon Bedrock serves as the enterprise multi-model control plane hosting Claude, GPT-5.5, and Grok 4.3. A new Cerebras-AWS partnership — announced alongside Cerebras Q1 2026 earnings on June 23 — pairs Cerebras CS-3 chips (high-speed decode) with AWS Trainium 3 (prefill) for inference disaggregation, deepening Bedrock's multi-silicon strategy.

items.headline: Cerebras and AWS announced a multi-year inference disaggregation partnership pairing CS-3 with Trainium 3
items.detail: Alongside its Q1 2026 earnings beat on June 23, Cerebras Systems announced a multi-year partnership with AWS that pairs Cerebras CS-3 chips (optimized for high-speed decode) with AWS Trainium 3 (optimized for prefill) in a disaggregated inference architecture on Amazon Bedrock. The partnership positions Bedrock as a multi-silicon inference runtime that routes workloads to the most cost-efficient hardware — Cerebras for ultra-fast token generation, Trainium for prefill — reinforcing AWS's platform-agnostic infrastructure strategy.

items.headline: AWS Summit New York concluded June 20 with AgentCore GA, AWS Continuum, AWS Context, and EC2 G7 instances
items.detail: AWS Summit New York (June 17–20) produced four flagship enterprise releases: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore GA (with Managed Knowledge Base, Web Search, Agent Optimization, A/B testing, intent analytics, and Guardrails), AWS Continuum (AI-native security for agentic workloads), AWS Context (knowledge graph for agents), and EC2 G7 instances with NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs delivering 4.6x AI inference performance.

items.headline: Amazon Bedrock is the enterprise multi-model control plane hosting Claude, GPT-5.5, and Grok 4.3 under unified governance
items.detail: Following AgentCore GA, Grok 4.3 on Bedrock (June 15), and OpenAI frontier models and Codex (June 1), Amazon Bedrock serves as the enterprise control plane where companies access Anthropic Claude, OpenAI GPT-5.5, and xAI Grok under unified AWS governance, compliance, billing, and security. The new Cerebras-AWS partnership adds a third silicon tier for ultra-fast token generation alongside NVIDIA and Trainium deployments.

companies.name: xAI
companies.summary: SpaceX (SPCX) fell approximately 16% on June 22 from its all-time high of $225.64, trading around $164 on June 23 — still up approximately 22% from its $135 June 12 IPO price. SpaceX signed a $6.3 billion compute deal with open-source AI startup Reflection AI, providing access to NVIDIA GB300s via the Colossus data center at $150 million per month starting July 1, 2026. The $60 billion all-stock Cursor (Anysphere) acquisition remains pending Q3 close. Grok for Microsoft Word (free add-in) and Grok 4.3 on Amazon Bedrock remain the latest product milestones.

items.headline: SpaceX signed $6.3B compute deal with Reflection AI via Colossus — $150M/month starting July 1
items.detail: SpaceX signed a $6.3 billion compute agreement with open-source AI startup Reflection AI (last valued at $25 billion, working with DoE Genesis Mission and Pentagon AI efforts), providing access to NVIDIA GB300 GPUs via the Colossus data center. Reflection will pay $150 million per month starting July 1, 2026 through 2029, with a 90-day termination clause after an initial 3-month period. The deal confirms Colossus operates as a multi-tenant commercial AI data center beyond xAI and Anthropic workloads.

items.headline: SPCX trading ~$164, down from $225.64 ATH; up ~22% from $135 IPO; Cursor acquisition pending Q3 close
items.detail: SpaceX (SPCX) fell approximately 16% on June 22 from its all-time high of $225.64 (reached June 16 following the Cursor announcement), trading around $164 on June 23 — still up approximately 22% from its $135 June 12 IPO price. The $60 billion all-stock acquisition of Anysphere (Cursor AI) — confirmed via SEC Form 8-K — remains pending regulatory approval and is expected to close Q3 2026. Cursor had over $2 billion ARR and more than half the Fortune 500 as clients.

items.headline: xAI launched Grok for Microsoft Word as a free add-in; Grok 4.3 GA on Amazon Bedrock
items.detail: xAI released Grok for Microsoft Word — a free Microsoft 365 add-in launched June 18 — that embeds Grok's conversational AI, real-time web search, and Mermaid-based diagram generation directly into Word's side panel. PowerPoint and Excel versions are also available. Grok 4.3 is generally available on Amazon Bedrock with a 1-million-token context window and configurable reasoning effort, and powers Gopuff's 'Go' shopping assistant and Databricks Agent Bricks.

companies.name: Broadcom
companies.category: AI networking, custom silicon, and infrastructure software
companies.summary: Apollo Global Management and Blackstone are leading a $35 billion capital solution for the Broadcom AI XPV Platform — described as the largest private financing ever executed — to enable more than 20 GW of compute capacity using Broadcom XPUs for Anthropic, OpenAI, and others through 2028. Apollo president Jim Zelter appeared on CNBC June 22 to detail the deal. Broadcom's $0.65 quarterly dividend is payable June 30 (7 days away). Q2 AI semiconductor revenue was $10.8 billion (+143% YoY); Q3 guidance is approximately $16 billion; FY2027 target exceeds $100 billion.

items.headline: Apollo and Blackstone leading $35B capital solution for Broadcom AI XPV Platform — enables 20GW+ compute for Anthropic, OpenAI, and others through 2028
items.detail: Apollo Global Management and Blackstone are leading a $35 billion capital solution for the Broadcom AI XPV Platform — described as the largest private financing ever executed — enabling more than 20 gigawatts of global AI compute capacity using Broadcom XPUs for hyperscalers including Anthropic, OpenAI, and others through 2028. Apollo president Jim Zelter appeared on CNBC June 22 to detail the deal. The platform positions Broadcom's custom silicon as an alternative to NVIDIA GPU clusters for hyperscaler-scale inference.

items.headline: Broadcom $0.65/share quarterly dividend payable June 30; Q2 AI revenue $10.8B, Q3 guidance ~$16B
items.detail: Broadcom's $0.65 per share quarterly cash dividend — marking 14 consecutive years of increases — is payable June 30 to holders of record June 22. Q2 FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue hit $10.8 billion (+143% YoY) on record quarterly revenue and free cash flow. Q3 AI semiconductor revenue guidance is approximately $16 billion. FY2026 target remains approximately $56 billion and FY2027 target exceeds $100 billion across six core custom-silicon customers including Anthropic, Google, and Meta.

items.headline: Broadcom Tomahawk 6 (Davisson): industry's first 102.4 Tbps Ethernet switch with co-packaged optics
items.detail: The Tomahawk 6 (codename Davisson), unveiled in mid-June 2026, is the industry's first 102.4 Tbps Ethernet switch silicon with co-packaged optics, addressing bandwidth demands of AI data centers where conventional pluggable optics are insufficient at hyperscale. JPMorgan maintains an 'aggressive buy' rating on AVGO; 48 analysts hold a 'Strong Buy' consensus with a $522 12-month price target.

companies.name: Palantir
companies.summary: Palantir won a U.S. Treasury Department contract to provide a common API layer supporting developer platforms, workflow automation, and data analytics for IT modernization. Palantir Q1 2026 revenue jumped 85% YoY to $1.6 billion with US commercial growth at 133%. Maven Smart System usage quadrupled over 12 months and retains DoD program-of-record status. The Anthropic-Palantir-AWS defense pipeline remains affected by the Fable/Mythos suspension; Trump's June 20 Axios statement has not produced a formal Commerce Department reversal.

items.headline: Palantir won U.S. Treasury Department contract for common API layer and IT modernization platform
items.detail: The U.S. Treasury Department awarded Palantir a contract to provide a common API layer supporting developer platforms, workflow automation, and data analytics for IT modernization. The win extends Palantir's federal footprint beyond defense into financial agency infrastructure. Earlier 2026 federal contracts include a $300 million USDA deal (April) and a $10 billion U.S. Army enterprise service agreement cap.

items.headline: Palantir Q1 revenue +85% YoY to $1.6B; US commercial +133%; Maven usage quadrupled; full-year guidance raised 10 points
items.detail: Palantir reported Q1 2026 revenue of $1.6 billion, up 85% year-over-year, with US commercial revenue growing 133% — the fastest commercial growth in company history. Maven Smart System usage quadrupled over the prior 12 months and retains its DoD program-of-record designation with multi-year protected funding. Palantir raised its full-year guidance by 10 points following the results. AIP is now available on Google Cloud Marketplace with BigQuery and Gemini integrations as an additional distribution channel.

items.headline: Anthropic-Palantir-AWS defense pipeline under strain; Maven still carrying Claude for eligible workflows
items.detail: Trump's June 20 Axios statement that Anthropic is no longer a national security threat has not produced a formal Commerce Department reversal. The Pentagon reportedly migrated at least two-thirds of its AI workflows off Anthropic's models during the supply chain dispute. Maven Smart System retains its DoD program-of-record designation, and Claude remains integrated in Maven for eligible workflows pending resolution. The new Treasury Department contract shows Palantir continuing to expand federal footprint independently of the Anthropic ban.

companies.name: Apple
companies.summary: Reporting indicates the Apple-Perplexity acquisition discussions are dead — Bloomberg's original report noted talks were early-stage and may not lead to an offer, Perplexity was never formally approached, and no offer was made. Apple's $1 billion per year Google Gemini deal powering a rebuilt Siri AI remains the defining Apple AI story with a September 2026 launch on Google Cloud NVIDIA Blackwell infrastructure. The iOS 27 Extensions framework (allowing ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini inside Siri) remains disabled. Bloomberg reports OpenAI is weighing a breach-of-contract notice over the perceived lack of prime ChatGPT placement.

items.headline: Apple-Perplexity acquisition discussions reported as dead — Perplexity was never formally approached
items.detail: Reporting on June 23 indicates the Apple-Perplexity acquisition discussions are dead. Bloomberg's original report noted the talks were early-stage and 'may not lead to an offer'; Perplexity management was never formally approached and the company confirmed it was unaware of M&A talks. No formal offer was made. CEO Aravind Srinivas has publicly stated a preference for a 2028 IPO. Apple's parallel consideration of Mistral AI — reported by Sherwood News — suggests the company is evaluating multiple AI strategy options beyond its existing OpenAI partnership.

items.headline: Apple's reported $1B/year Gemini deal powers rebuilt Siri AI launching September 2026; iOS 27 Extensions disabled
items.detail: Apple's reported $1 billion per year deal for Google Gemini to power a rebuilt Siri AI — announced at WWDC 2026 (June 8) — remains active with a September 2026 launch target on Google Cloud NVIDIA Blackwell infrastructure. The iOS 27 developer beta contains a working Extensions framework allowing users to choose ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini inside Siri, but Apple has disabled it pending DMA regulatory clarity. Bloomberg reports OpenAI is weighing a breach-of-contract notice over perceived lack of prime ChatGPT placement.

companies.name: AMD
companies.category: AI accelerators, CPUs, and data center silicon
companies.summary: AMD shares fell 6.3% on June 23 after reports that SK Hynix is slowing HBM4 expansion to redirect capacity to conventional DRAM — a margin concern for AMD's MI450 roadmap, not a demand destruction signal per analysts. AMD announced it powers 4 of the 10 most powerful supercomputers in the world. MI450 GPU samples continue shipping to lead customers OpenAI and Meta. AMD Advancing AI 2026 is July 22–23 in San Francisco. AMD reports Q2 2026 results August 4.

items.headline: AMD fell 6.3% on June 23 after SK Hynix slows HBM4 expansion — analysts call it a margin story, not demand destruction
items.detail: AMD shares fell 6.3% on June 23 after reports that SK Hynix is slowing HBM4 memory capacity expansion to redirect production to conventional DRAM. Analysts characterized the sell-off as a margin concern for AMD's MI450 GPU roadmap rather than evidence of weakening AI GPU demand. AMD CEO Lisa Su previously confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to OpenAI and Meta, with Helios rack systems on target for H2 2026 production. The MEXT acquisition (completed June 15) brings predictive memory technology making flash storage behave like DRAM for AI workloads.

items.headline: AMD powers 4 of 10 most powerful supercomputers; MI450 samples shipping to OpenAI and Meta
items.detail: AMD announced on June 23 that its chips power 4 of the 10 most powerful supercomputers in the world. AMD CEO Lisa Su confirmed MI450 GPUs are sampling to lead customers including OpenAI and Meta. AMD denied reports of MI455X delays, stating Helios rack systems — AMD's Vera Rubin competitor — remain on target for H2 2026 production. The Rackspace 30 MW AMD AI Compute deployment (signed June 16) adds cloud capacity for MI450-class workloads.

items.headline: AMD Advancing AI 2026 is July 22–23 in San Francisco; Q2 results August 4
items.detail: AMD's Advancing AI 2026 event is a two-day in-person and livestreamed event on July 22–23 at Moscone Center in San Francisco — the next major disclosure milestone. Expected disclosures include formal MI450 series launch, EPYC Venice Zen 6 CPUs, Helios rack confirmation, and a preview of the MI500 series targeting 2027. AMD reports Q2 2026 financial results on August 4.

companies.name: Perplexity AI
companies.summary: Reports indicate the Apple-Perplexity acquisition discussions are dead — Perplexity management was never formally approached and no offer was made. CEO Aravind Srinivas maintains the 2028 IPO target. Perplexity's $500M ARR (April 2026, 335% YoY growth), 100M+ MAU, and $20B valuation from the May 2026 round remain the current price anchors. Perplexity Computer is live as native side panels in Microsoft 365 apps; the Comet AI browser reached #3 in the App Store within 48 hours of its iOS launch.

items.headline: Apple-Perplexity acquisition discussions reported as dead; CEO maintains 2028 IPO target
items.detail: Reporting on June 23 indicates the Apple-Perplexity acquisition discussions are dead. Perplexity management was never formally approached, the company was unaware of M&A talks, and no formal offer was made. CEO Aravind Srinivas confirmed the 2028 IPO target on June 9 as a deliberate strategy regardless of Anthropic and OpenAI moving toward public markets. ARR reached $500 million in April 2026 (335% YoY growth) with 100M+ monthly active users and a $20B valuation from the May 2026 round.

items.headline: Perplexity Computer live as native Microsoft 365 side panels; Comet browser reached #3 App Store on iOS launch
items.detail: Perplexity's Computer agentic tool is live as native side panels in Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Teams — directly competing with Microsoft Copilot inside Microsoft 365. The Comet AI browser is globally available and reached the #3 App Store position within 48 hours of its iOS launch, positioning Perplexity as a workplace AI layer and browser-level intelligence platform.

companies.name: CoreWeave
companies.summary: CoreWeave (CRWV) joined the Nasdaq-100 on June 22 and closed +9.67% at $117.03. Russell 3000 inclusion follows June 27 — 4 days away — alongside IREN Limited. Cerebras Q1 2026 earnings (released June 23) beat massively at $193.4M revenue vs $56.65M estimate, including a $20B+ OpenAI compute deal, confirming accelerating AI inference demand that validates CoreWeave's $131B backlog. The 2026 capex floor was raised to $31 billion.

items.headline: CoreWeave closed +9.67% at $117.03 on Nasdaq-100 inclusion day (June 22); Russell 3000 follows June 27
items.detail: CoreWeave (CRWV) officially joined the Nasdaq-100 Index before market open on June 22 — just 15 months after its IPO at $40 per share — and closed at $117.03, up 9.67%, as mandatory buying from QQQ and Nasdaq-100 tracking funds (representing $662B+ in tracked assets) executed. Russell 3000 inclusion is effective after close June 26 (trading June 27), alongside IREN Limited. Other Nasdaq-100 additions June 22: Astera Labs, Nebius Group, Rocket Lab, and Teradyne.

items.headline: Cerebras Q1 2026 earnings beat (June 23) confirms accelerating AI inference demand — $20B+ OpenAI deal validates the CoreWeave demand thesis
items.detail: Cerebras Q1 2026 revenue came in at $193.4 million — far above the $56.65M analyst estimate — with a new $20B+ OpenAI compute deal (750MW inference capacity) and a multi-year AWS partnership announced alongside earnings. The results confirm that AI inference demand is accelerating rapidly, validating CoreWeave's $31 billion 2026 capex and approaching $131 billion backlog. CoreWeave's competing Vera Rubin NVL72 capacity serves many of the same customers as Cerebras (OpenAI, Meta, Anthropic).

items.headline: CoreWeave backlog approaching $131B; 2026 capex floor $31B; Vera Rubin NVL72 bring-up completed June 1
items.detail: CoreWeave's contracted revenue backlog is approaching approximately $131 billion, up roughly $32 billion since reporting $99.4 billion at end-Q1 2026. The 2026 capex floor was raised to $31 billion to accommodate Vera Rubin NVL72 commitments from Meta, Anthropic, and OpenAI. CoreWeave completed the industry-first Vera Rubin NVL72 system bring-up on June 1. A new $2.2 billion Chicago-area data-center lease adds contracted revenue visibility.

companies.name: Mistral AI
companies.summary: Mistral AI confirmed a $3.5 billion raise at a €20 billion valuation. Its Paris-area data center at Bruyères-le-Châtel (44 MW, 13,800–18,000 NVIDIA GB300 GPU systems, operated by Eclairion) is expected to open by end of June 2026 — no opening announcement found as of June 23. The NVIDIA-Mistral Mistral Compute sovereign AI GPU cloud launched at VivaTech June 20. Mistral launched Vibe — a unified AI agent for work and code. CEO Arthur Mensch continues to leverage the Fable ban to position Mistral as the open-source sovereignty alternative.

items.headline: Mistral Paris data center still 'end of June' — no opening announcement as of June 23
items.detail: Mistral AI's Bruyères-le-Châtel data center near Paris (44 MW, 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 GPU systems, operated by Eclairion) is expected to open by end of June 2026, but no opening announcement was found as of June 23. A secondary Les Ulis (Essonne) 10 MW inference facility is scheduled for Q3 2026. The Bruyères-le-Châtel facility forms the first node in a planned 200 MW European compute roadmap and is the flagship of the Mistral Compute sovereign AI cloud platform launched at VivaTech June 20.

items.headline: NVIDIA and Mistral's Mistral Compute sovereign AI GPU cloud launched at VivaTech; Vibe agent launched; Emmi AI acquired
items.detail: NVIDIA and Mistral AI launched Mistral Compute — a sovereign AI GPU cloud platform for European customers and governments — at VivaTech 2026 (closing June 20). Jensen Huang highlighted Mistral Compute as Europe's AI sovereignty infrastructure. Mistral also launched Vibe, a unified AI agent for work and code combining Work Mode and Code Mode with a VS Code extension and CLI. Mistral acquired Austrian company Emmi AI, which develops AI simulation models for industrial engineering.

items.headline: CEO Mensch positions Mistral as open-source sovereignty alternative to US-controlled models amid Fable ban
items.detail: CEO Arthur Mensch is actively leveraging European concern over the Anthropic Fable/Mythos export suspension to position Mistral as the open-source alternative that governments cannot revoke. Mensch stated: 'We exist to make sure that everyone gets access to the best AI systems, outside of centralised control exercised by states or corporations.' Mistral targets €1 billion in 2026 revenue with 1,000 employees.

companies.name: Tesla
companies.category: Autonomous vehicles, robotics, and applied AI
companies.summary: No major new Tesla announcements on June 23. Cybercab production continues at Giga Texas with 102+ units in the outbound lot; the EPA filing (June 19) confirmed full vehicle specs. The unsupervised robotaxi service covers Austin (full metro expanded June 8), Dallas, Houston, and the SF Bay Area. Optimus Gen 3 is in mass production at Fremont with V3 full-body production targeting summer 2026. FSD v14.3 reduces inference latency 20%.

items.headline: Cybercab production ramp: 102+ units in Giga Texas; robotaxi service expanded to full Austin metro
items.detail: As of June 19, 102 Cybercab units were in the outbound lot at Giga Texas. The EPA filing confirmed full vehicle specifications: 47.6 kWh battery, 163 kW motor, 418-mile unadjusted range (293-mile adjusted), and 3,113 lb curb weight. The unsupervised robotaxi service expanded to cover the entire Austin metro area (June 8) and is also active in Dallas, Houston, and the SF Bay Area. Both a Cybercab and a Cybertruck have been spotted with specialized robotaxi validation hardware, indicating Tesla is validating autonomous ride-hailing on multiple vehicle platforms.

items.headline: Optimus Gen 3 in mass production at Fremont; dedicated Optimus factory at Giga Texas North Campus targeting 10M robots/year
items.detail: Tesla's Optimus Gen 3 humanoid robot entered mass production at Fremont in January 2026, with V3 full-body production targeting summer 2026. Tesla's Q1 2026 report confirmed a 5.2 million square foot Optimus factory expansion at Gigafactory Texas North Campus targeting 10 million robots per year. FSD (Supervised) v14.3 launched in April 2026 with a rewritten AI compiler reducing inference latency by up to 20%. Public Optimus sales target end of 2027 at $20,000–$30,000.

ipoTracker.status: Confirmed filing; Fable/Mythos ban day 11; credit billing at $10/$50 per million tokens live June 23; science event June 30; 57% odds of US restoration by July 1
ipoTracker.process: Anthropic confirmed its confidential draft Form S-1 submission to the SEC on June 1, 2026, after closing a $65 billion Series H at a ~$965 billion post-money valuation (May 28). An October 2026 listing window is widely reported. As of June 23, Fable 5 and Mythos 5 remain offline globally (day 11). The credit-based pricing model at $10/$50 per million input/output tokens went live today. Anthropic confirmed 'The Briefing: AI for Science' virtual event for June 30. Nobel laureate John Jumper (AlphaFold co-creator) joined Anthropic. Trump told Axios (June 20) he no longer views Anthropic as a national security threat, but no formal Commerce Department reversal has been issued. Anthropic's July 8 privacy policy update creates a biometric ID verification path for US citizen access. Revenue run rate surpasses $30 billion.
ipoTracker.note: The June 23 credit billing activation and June 30 science event are the key near-term milestones. Watch July 8 (biometric ID policy effective) as the next structured restoration pathway. August 1 is the 60-day executive order deadline for NSA/Treasury/CISA AI risk framework.

latestArticle.title: Anthropic confirms 'The Briefing: AI for Science' virtual event June 30

ipoTracker.status: Confirmed SEC filing June 8; GPT-5.6 unreleased June 23 despite high prediction market odds; 'kindle-alpha' briefly spotted; 42-state AG probe; CFO may push to 2027
ipoTracker.process: OpenAI confirmed its confidential S-1 filing with the SEC on June 8, 2026. Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and JPMorgan are leading. CEO Sam Altman expects a listing within the next year; CFO Sarah Friar has reportedly expressed concern about operational readiness, with a potential delay to 2027. A coalition of 42 state attorneys general (led by NY AG Letitia James) opened a formal probe on June 12. GPT-5.6 did not launch on June 23 despite 83–90% prediction market odds for a June 22–28 release; a release candidate codenamed 'kindle-alpha' was briefly visible on Design Arena before being pulled. EU AI Act chatbot enforcement begins August 2, 2026.
ipoTracker.note: GPT-5.6 is the next major product catalyst — 'kindle-alpha' on Design Arena is the strongest unofficial signal of imminent release. 42-state AG probe and potential 2027 IPO delay are the key pre-IPO risk factors.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI's CFO reportedly wants to delay the IPO from 2026 to 2027

ipoTracker.status: SPCX trading ~$164, down from $225.64 ATH; up ~22% from $135 IPO; $60B Cursor acquisition pending Q3 close; Reflection AI $6.3B compute deal signed
ipoTracker.process: SpaceX priced at $135/share on June 11, raising $75 billion at a $1.75 trillion valuation in the largest IPO ever recorded. Shares peaked at $225.64 on June 16 following the Cursor announcement, then fell approximately 16% on June 22, trading around $164 on June 23 — still up approximately 22% from the IPO price. SpaceX confirmed via SEC Form 8-K the $60B all-stock acquisition of Anysphere (Cursor AI), expected to close Q3 2026. SpaceX also signed a $6.3B compute deal with Reflection AI via Colossus ($150M/month starting July 1). xAI was folded into SpaceX as the SpaceXAI division in February 2026; all Grok/AI exposure trades via ticker SPCX.
ipoTracker.confidence: Confirmed; public company as of June 12, 2026
ipoTracker.note: SPCX price pullback from $225.64 ATH to ~$164 reflects post-Cursor consolidation. Reflection AI compute deal ($6.3B) confirms Colossus is now a multi-tenant commercial platform. Cursor Q3 close remains the next major milestone.

latestArticle.title: SpaceX signed $6.3B compute deal with Reflection AI via Colossus

ipoTracker.status: IPO completed May 2026; Q1 2026 earnings massively beat June 23 — $193.4M vs $56.65M estimate; $20B+ OpenAI compute deal; AWS partnership; FY2026 guidance $855–865M; stock -8% extended hours on margin guidance
ipoTracker.process: Cerebras completed its Nasdaq IPO in May 2026 under ticker CBRS. Q1 2026 results released after market close June 23 massively beat analyst estimates: revenue $193.4M (vs $56.65M expected, +92–94% YoY); GAAP gross margin 45%; FY2026 core revenue guidance $855.5M–$865M (69% growth at midpoint). Two major deals announced alongside earnings: a multi-year OpenAI compute deal (750MW, $20B+ valued, with option to $30B) and a multi-year AWS partnership pairing Cerebras CS-3 (decode) with AWS Trainium 3 (prefill) for inference disaggregation. Stock fell approximately 8% in after-hours trading on forward gross margin compression guidance.
ipoTracker.note: Q1 2026 revenue more than 3x analyst estimates — one of the most significant AI infrastructure earnings surprises of 2026. The $20B+ OpenAI deal confirms Cerebras as a Tier 1 AI compute provider. After-hours stock decline on margin compression guidance is the key risk to watch.

latestArticle.title: Cerebras Systems Announces Strong First Quarter 2026 Results

ipoTracker.status: CRWV in Nasdaq-100 as of June 22; closed +9.67% at $117.03; Russell 3000 effective June 27 (4 days away); backlog ~$131B; capex floor $31B
ipoTracker.process: CoreWeave completed its IPO in March 2025 (priced at $40/share) and trades on Nasdaq as CRWV. The stock joined the Nasdaq-100 on June 22 and closed at $117.03 (+9.67%) as mandatory fund buying executed. Russell 3000 inclusion is effective after close June 26 (trading June 27), alongside IREN Limited. Q1 2026 revenue was $2.1 billion (+112% YoY). Contracted backlog is approaching approximately $131 billion. 2026 capex floor raised to $31 billion for Vera Rubin NVL72 buildout serving Meta, Anthropic, and OpenAI.
ipoTracker.note: Russell 3000 inclusion effective June 27 — 4 days away. Cerebras Q1 2026 earnings beat (June 23) confirms the AI inference demand backdrop driving CoreWeave's backlog growth. OpenAI's $20B+ Cerebras deal shows OpenAI is diversifying compute across multiple platforms beyond CoreWeave.

latestArticle.title: CoreWeave (CRWV) and IREN join Russell 3000 Index

ipoTracker.status: No filing; IPO deferred to 2027 or later; $134B valuation, $5.4B ARR at 65% YoY growth
ipoTracker.process: Databricks CEO Ali Ghodsi reiterated in early June that going public is 'absolutely not happening in 2026,' citing the SpaceX/Anthropic/OpenAI trifecta absorbing market capital. The company has a $5.4 billion annualized revenue run-rate growing 65% YoY with positive free cash flow and a $134 billion valuation from its December 2025 Series L. The 2026 Data + AI Summit (June 18) featured SpaceXAI announcing native Grok availability on Databricks Agent Bricks.
ipoTracker.note: 2027+ IPO window unchanged. No new developments June 23.

latestArticle.title: Databricks CEO says company plans IPO despite current market conditions

Scale raised more than $14 billion in June 2025 and is unlikely to pursue a near-term IPO.

ipoTracker.status: No confirmed filing; Apple acquisition talks reported dead; Perplexity was never formally approached; CEO reaffirmed 2028 IPO target
ipoTracker.process: Reporting on June 23 indicates the Apple-Perplexity acquisition discussions are dead — Perplexity management was never formally approached, no formal offer was made, and the company confirmed it was unaware of M&A talks. CEO Aravind Srinivas publicly stated a preference for an independent 2028 IPO on June 9. ARR reached $500 million (April 2026, 335% YoY growth), with 100M+ monthly active users and a $20B valuation from the May 2026 round.
ipoTracker.confidence: No filing; Apple acquisition reported dead; 2028 IPO remains stated preference
ipoTracker.note: Apple acquisition talks now reported dead — the 2028 IPO path is the primary trajectory. Apple's consideration of both Perplexity and Mistral suggests Apple is still evaluating non-OpenAI AI strategy options.

Pre-IPO trading platform listings quoting prices inconsistent with the claimed $42 IPO price directly contradict the claim.

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed compute partnership; Fable/Mythos ban does not affect compute terms; Colossus confirmed multi-tenant with new Reflection AI deal
partnershipTracker.terms: SpaceXAI (AI division of publicly traded SpaceX/SPCX) signed an agreement to provide Anthropic access to Colossus 1 compute capacity — operating 220,000 H100s with 300 MW. Anthropic expressed interest in developing multiple gigawatts of orbital AI compute capacity. Public financial terms remain undisclosed. The Fable 5/Mythos 5 export suspension affects model distribution but not the underlying compute contract. SpaceX also signed a separate $6.3B compute deal with Reflection AI via Colossus, confirming multi-tenant commercial operation of the data center.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed relationship; financial terms not confirmed
partnershipTracker.note: Colossus's Reflection AI deal ($6.3B, $150M/month starting July 1) confirms multi-tenant commercial operation. SPCX trading ~$164 after pullback from $225.64 ATH. No new Anthropic-specific terms.

latestArticle.title: New Compute Partnership with Anthropic

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed expanded collaboration; AgentCore GA deepens enterprise layer; Cerebras-AWS inference disaggregation adds complementary silicon tier to Bedrock
partnershipTracker.terms: The April 2026 expanded agreement commits more than $100 billion over 10 years, securing up to 5GW of Trainium2, Trainium3, and Trainium4 capacity. Project Rainier already runs more than one million Trainium2 chips for Claude workloads. AgentCore GA at AWS Summit NYC incorporates Managed Knowledge Base, Web Search, Agent Optimization, and Guardrails as the enterprise deployment layer for Claude on Bedrock. Amazon confirmed Claude models (outside Fable 5 and Mythos 5) remain available to AWS customers. The new Cerebras-AWS partnership (June 23) adds Cerebras CS-3 as a complementary silicon tier for ultra-fast decode alongside Trainium.
partnershipTracker.note: Cerebras-AWS inference disaggregation partnership (June 23) reinforces Bedrock's multi-silicon strategy and indirectly benefits Anthropic's Bedrock distribution channel. No new Anthropic-AWS contract terms.

latestArticle.title: Anthropic and Amazon expand collaboration for up to 5 gigawatts of new compute

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed compute expansion; Apollo/Blackstone $35B Broadcom AI XPV Platform adds financing layer for custom silicon
partnershipTracker.terms: Anthropic's agreement with Google and Broadcom secures approximately 3.5 gigawatts of next-generation TPU capacity expected to start coming online in 2027. Google has invested up to $40 billion in Anthropic (April 24). Broadcom reconfirmed Anthropic as one of its six core custom-silicon customers. Apollo and Blackstone are leading a $35 billion capital solution for the Broadcom AI XPV Platform to enable 20GW+ compute capacity for hyperscalers including Anthropic through 2028. The Fable/Mythos suspension does not affect this infrastructure agreement.
partnershipTracker.note: The Apollo/Blackstone $35B Broadcom AI XPV Platform (announced June 9, highlighted June 22) is the most significant new financing development for this relationship — it creates a structured capital vehicle to scale Broadcom XPU capacity for Anthropic and others through 2028.

latestArticle.title: Apollo leads $35 billion capital solution for Broadcom AI XPV Platform

partnershipTracker.type: Cloud, model distribution, investment, and GPU/CPU optimization
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed strategic partnerships; Claude models in Foundry public preview; Microsoft IQ GA; NVIDIA Annual Meeting June 24
partnershipTracker.terms: Anthropic has committed to purchase $30 billion of Azure compute capacity (with option for 1GW more). Claude Sonnet 4.5, Haiku 4.5, and Opus 4.1 are in public preview in Microsoft Foundry. NVIDIA's Vera CPUs are in production at Anthropic. Microsoft IQ is GA across Foundry, enabling richer enterprise agent deployments. The Fable/Mythos suspension does not affect other Claude models in Foundry. NVIDIA's Annual Meeting is tomorrow June 24.
partnershipTracker.note: NVIDIA Annual Meeting tomorrow (June 24) may include infrastructure update disclosures. Microsoft's exploration of open-source model alternatives for Copilot Cowork does not affect Claude's position in Foundry.

latestArticle.title: Vera Arrives: NVIDIA's First CPU Built for Agents Lands at Top AI Labs

partnershipTracker.type: Cloud, product distribution, and IP licensing
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed amended partnership; Microsoft exploring open-source model alternatives for some Copilot workloads; July 1 Copilot Cowork deadline 8 days away
partnershipTracker.terms: Microsoft remains OpenAI's primary cloud partner under a non-exclusive IP license through 2032. GPT-5.5 reached general availability in Microsoft Foundry on June 3. Microsoft IQ went GA June 16. OpenAI's Astral acquisition (June 19) will integrate Python developer tooling into the Codex ecosystem on Azure. Microsoft is exploring open-source model alternatives for some Copilot Cowork workloads. Copilot Cowork usage-based billing active since June 19 with July 1 grace period (8 days away).
partnershipTracker.note: July 1 Copilot Cowork grace period deadline is 8 days away. Microsoft's DeepSeek exploration remains the key tension point.

latestArticle.title: The next phase of the Microsoft OpenAI partnership

partnershipTracker.type: Cloud, model distribution, and strategic infrastructure partnership
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed strategic partnership — GA distribution live; AgentCore GA reinforces Bedrock as enterprise runtime; Cerebras-AWS deal adds compute complementarity
partnershipTracker.terms: OpenAI's frontier models (GPT-5.5, GPT-5.4) and Codex are generally available on AWS Bedrock since June 1. OpenAI also holds a 2GW Trainium capacity contract. AgentCore GA provides an enterprise agent runtime hosting OpenAI-powered agents alongside Claude and Grok on Bedrock. The new Cerebras-AWS inference disaggregation partnership (June 23) adds a third silicon path for OpenAI workloads on Bedrock.
partnershipTracker.note: The Cerebras-OpenAI $20B+ compute deal (announced June 23) represents an additional compute relationship for OpenAI beyond AWS Trainium and NVIDIA — OpenAI is diversifying silicon supply across NVIDIA, AMD, Trainium, and now Cerebras.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS

partnershipTracker.type: GPU infrastructure and strategic supplier relationship
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed strategic partnership — Vera CPUs in production at OpenAI; H2 2026 cloud deployment on track; NVIDIA Annual Meeting June 24
partnershipTracker.terms: NVIDIA agreed to deploy at least 10GW of NVIDIA systems for OpenAI infrastructure, with the first gigawatt targeted for H2 2026 on Vera Rubin. NVIDIA's Vera CPUs are now in production at OpenAI. The new Cerebras-OpenAI $20B+ compute deal does not affect NVIDIA's role as OpenAI's primary GPU supplier for training and large-scale inference.
partnershipTracker.note: NVIDIA Annual Meeting tomorrow (June 24) may include Vera Rubin production ramp disclosures. H2 2026 cloud deployments remain the next major milestone.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI and NVIDIA Announce Strategic Partnership to Deploy 10 Gigawatts of NVIDIA Systems

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed strategic partnership — MI450 samples shipping to OpenAI; H2 2026 deployment on track despite AMD stock decline June 23
partnershipTracker.terms: OpenAI and AMD agreed on a multi-generation 6GW AMD GPU deployment beginning with an initial MI450 rollout in H2 2026. AMD CEO confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to OpenAI. A 160-million-share AMD warrant vests on milestone achievement. AMD's stock fell 6.3% on June 23 due to SK Hynix HBM4 capacity redirection — a margin concern, not a demand destruction signal per analysts.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by company; samples shipping per AMD CEO
partnershipTracker.note: AMD's 6.3% stock decline on June 23 (SK Hynix HBM4 news) is a margin concern, not evidence of AMD-OpenAI deployment delays. H2 2026 production timeline remains on track.

latestArticle.title: AMD and OpenAI announce strategic partnership to deploy 6 gigawatts of AMD GPUs

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed platform; CoreWeave in Nasdaq-100; Russell 3000 June 27; Cerebras $20B+ deal shows OpenAI diversifying compute beyond Stargate
partnershipTracker.terms: Stargate spans Oracle (up to 4.5GW, $300B+ over five years), SoftBank (two sites scaling to 1.5GW), and CoreWeave (cloud partnership totaling up to $22.4 billion across three expansions). CoreWeave's backlog approaches $131 billion and 2026 capex floor was raised to $31 billion. The new Cerebras-OpenAI $20B+ compute deal (June 23) demonstrates OpenAI also building inference capacity outside the Stargate network — Cerebras CS-3 chips complement rather than replace Stargate's GPU infrastructure for specific high-speed decode workloads.
partnershipTracker.note: Cerebras-OpenAI $20B+ deal (June 23) is significant: OpenAI is building a heterogeneous compute supply chain — Stargate (NVIDIA/Oracle/CoreWeave) for training and large inference, Cerebras for ultra-fast decode, AMD for inference diversification.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI, Oracle, and SoftBank expand Stargate with five new AI data center sites

partnershipTracker.status: New confirmed partnership — announced June 23 alongside Cerebras Q1 2026 earnings; $20B+ for 750MW inference capacity
partnershipTracker.terms: Cerebras Systems announced alongside its Q1 2026 earnings (June 23) a multi-year compute deal with OpenAI for 750 megawatts of inference capacity valued at more than $20 billion, with an option to scale to $30 billion. Cerebras CS-3 chips — which achieve the lowest reported token latency in the industry — will provide ultra-fast decode for OpenAI inference workloads, complementing OpenAI's existing NVIDIA, AMD, and Trainium capacity. Full financial terms and timeline details were not disclosed.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by Cerebras alongside Q1 2026 earnings
partnershipTracker.note: This is the most significant new partnership announced June 23. The $20B+ size puts Cerebras on par with major compute contracts signed by CoreWeave with Meta ($21B). OpenAI now has confirmed multi-vendor silicon diversity: NVIDIA (10GW), AMD (6GW), Trainium (2GW via AWS), and Cerebras (750MW ultra-fast decode).

latestArticle.title: Cerebras Systems Announces Strong First Quarter 2026 Results

partnershipTracker.terms: CoreWeave provides Meta AI cloud capacity through December 2032 under a roughly $21 billion agreement, including NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 deployments for Meta's Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp inference workloads. This contract is among the anchor commitments driving CoreWeave's backlog approaching $131 billion. Meta's in-house Meta Glasses launch (June 23) adds new AI inference workloads that will scale into this infrastructure.
partnershipTracker.note: Meta Glasses launch (June 23) adds new AI inference demand for the Meta-CoreWeave infrastructure relationship. Russell 3000 inclusion effective June 27. No new contract terms.

CoreWeave's approaching $131 billion backlog includes the Anthropic contract; Vera Rubin NVL72 is the key enabler for future Claude deployments.
partnershipTracker.note: Russell 3000 inclusion effective June 27 — 4 days away. The Fable/Mythos suspension is a regulatory issue; the CoreWeave compute contract and infrastructure rollout are unaffected.

partnershipTracker.terms: Claude runs inside Palantir's Maven Smart System on AWS infrastructure for US government intelligence and defense users. The Pentagon reportedly migrated at least two-thirds of its AI workflows off Anthropic's models amid the supply chain risk designation dispute. Maven Smart System retains its DoD program-of-record designation. Trump's June 20 Axios statement is the most positive government signal yet, but no formal Commerce Department reversal has been issued. Palantir's new Treasury Department contract (June 23) shows continued federal expansion independently of the Anthropic ban.

partnershipTracker.note: Palantir's Treasury Department contract (June 23) expands its federal footprint independent of the Anthropic situation. Watch for formal Commerce Department action following Trump's June 20 Axios statement.

partnershipTracker.status: Strained — Gemini deal sidelined OpenAI placement; OpenAI weighing breach-of-contract notice; Perplexity acquisition talks dead; iOS 27 Extensions disabled
partnershipTracker.terms: Apple and OpenAI maintain an existing integration bringing ChatGPT into Siri and Apple Intelligence. Apple's reported $1B/year Google Gemini deal (announced WWDC June 8) effectively displaced OpenAI as the primary Siri AI engine. The iOS 27 developer beta contains a working Extensions framework letting users choose ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini inside Siri, but Apple has disabled it. Bloomberg reports OpenAI is weighing a breach-of-contract notice. Apple-Perplexity acquisition discussions are now reported as dead.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed integration; breach-of-contract claim reported but not confirmed
partnershipTracker.note: Perplexity acquisition talks reported dead (June 23) — Apple's AI strategy is now more clearly the Gemini deal for Siri, with OpenAI in a strained secondary position. OpenAI's potential breach-of-contract notice remains the key pending legal development.

latestArticle.title: Apple hides Siri Extensions — third-party AI selection missing from WWDC

partnershipTracker.type: AI model integration — Gemini powering Siri AI
partnershipTracker.status: Active — $1B/year deal; Siri AI launching September 2026; Gemini 3.5 Pro 7 days to June 30 deadline; Perplexity acquisition dead makes Gemini deal more strategically central
partnershipTracker.terms: Apple struck a reported $1 billion per year deal for Google Gemini to power a rebuilt Siri AI, announced at WWDC 2026 (June 8). Siri AI will run on Google Cloud infrastructure powered by NVIDIA Blackwell chips, launching September 2026. The EU launch is delayed pending unresolved Digital Markets Act regulatory talks. With Apple-Perplexity acquisition talks now reported as dead, the Gemini deal becomes Apple's primary AI model distribution strategy. Gemini 3.5 Pro — 7 days from its June 30 deadline — is the likely model for the next Siri AI integration wave.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Reported by credible outlets; not officially confirmed by Apple or Google
partnershipTracker.note: Perplexity acquisition dead (June 23) makes the Apple-Google Gemini deal more strategically central — Apple now has fewer credible alternatives to Gemini for Siri AI. Gemini 3.5 Pro's June 30 deadline is 7 days away.

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed partnership — Mistral Compute launched VivaTech June 20; Paris data center still 'end of June'; no opening announcement as of June 23
partnershipTracker.terms: NVIDIA and Mistral AI launched Mistral Compute — a sovereign AI GPU cloud platform for European customers and governments — at VivaTech 2026 (closing June 20). Mistral's Bruyères-le-Châtel data center near Paris (44 MW, 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 systems, operated by Eclairion) is expected to open by end of June 2026 — no opening announcement found as of June 23. Plans include a 200 MW European compute roadmap.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by both companies at VivaTech
partnershipTracker.note: Paris data center opening expected end of June — 7 days or fewer away. No announcement as of June 23.

latestArticle.title: France Advances Europe's AI Future With NVIDIA Technologies

partnershipTracker.type: AI compute contract — Colossus data center access
partnershipTracker.status: New confirmed partnership — $6.3B compute deal signed June 22; $150M/month starting July 1, 2026
partnershipTracker.terms: SpaceX signed a $6.3 billion compute agreement with open-source AI startup Reflection AI (last valued at $25 billion, working with DoE Genesis Mission and Pentagon AI efforts), providing access to NVIDIA GB300 GPUs via the Colossus data center. Reflection will pay $150 million per month starting July 1, 2026 through 2029, with a 90-day termination clause after an initial 3-month period. The deal confirms Colossus operates as a multi-tenant commercial AI data center.
partnershipTracker.note: New partnership confirmed June 22–23. At $150M/month, Reflection AI's commitment represents $6.3B over the term — the same order of magnitude as the Anthropic-CoreWeave and Meta-CoreWeave contracts. Confirms Colossus is now a commercially operated multi-tenant platform.

latestArticle.title: SpaceX AI Colossus data center signs deal with Reflection AI

partnershipTracker.type: Structured finance platform for Broadcom AI XPU deployment
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed — $35B capital solution; largest private financing ever executed; enables 20GW+ custom silicon compute through 2028
partnershipTracker.terms: Apollo Global Management and Blackstone are leading a $35 billion capital solution for the Broadcom AI XPV Platform, enabling more than 20 gigawatts of global AI compute capacity using Broadcom XPUs for hyperscalers including Anthropic, OpenAI, and others through 2028. Apollo president Jim Zelter appeared on CNBC June 22 to detail the deal, which was described as the largest private financing ever executed.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by Apollo press release and Broadcom announcement
partnershipTracker.note: The $35B platform creates a capital vehicle that lets hyperscalers access Broadcom XPU capacity without bearing the full capex burden — directly competing with NVIDIA GPU clusters funded through similar capital structures.

latestArticle.title: Apollo leads $35 billion capital solution for Broadcom AI XPV Platform
